function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'printf' to reach function 'main'?
loop_reachability: Can the loop with condition 'c <= 'z'' be skipped entirely?
Is the loop with condition 'c <= 'z'' always executed?
Is it possible that the loop with condition 'c <= 'Z'' is never executed?
Is the loop with condition 'c <= 'Z'' always executed?
dominators: Is it guaranteed that whenever line 2:  <statement> int main() </statement> is reached, the statement line 6:  <statement> c='A';while( c <= 'Z' ) { printf("%d - %c\n",c,c); c++; } </statement> has already been reached?

Does the statement line 6:  <statement> c='A';while( c <= 'Z' ) { printf("%d - %c\n",c,c); c++; } </statement> dominate line 7:  <statement> c='0';while( c <= '9' ) { printf("%d - %c\n",c,c); c++; } </statement>?
liveness: Does variable 'c' remain in use at the end of function 'main'?
dead_code: Is this statement not dead: “c='a';while( c <= 'z' ) { printf("%d - %c\n",c,c); c++; }”?
